steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the main purpose of the passage. The passage opens two opposing critical camps — one finding fault, one defending — and presents both without resolution. The organizing principle is that contrast.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'O'Brian's intentional structural design': Frames the content as O'Brian's own choice rather than a critics' debate. The passage focuses on what critics argue, not on authorial intent. ✗
  • Choice B — 'critics with differing views on the books' structural efficacy': Captures the passage's core: two sides disagreeing over whether the abrupt endings succeed or fail as a structural choice. ✓
  • Choice C — 'comparing the series to the Forsyte Saga as a standard of renown': The Forsyte Saga appears as an analogy for how to read the series (as one long work), not as a benchmark of literary prestige. ✗
  • Choice D — 'praising the series for entertainment value': The passage never praises the books for entertainment — that claim is invented. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
